# CWE-257 positive: reversible AES encryption for passwords
lang: go
file: CWE-257-vulnerable.go
---
package sample

import (
	"crypto/aes"
	"crypto/cipher"
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/base64"
	"io"
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// CWE-257 Storing Passwords in a Recoverable Format
// Vulnerable line: encrypts password with static key for later decryption.
func StoreRecoverablePassword(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB, key []byte) {
	plain := []byte(c.PostForm("password"))
	block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
	if err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "crypto"})
		return
	}
	gcm, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
	if err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "crypto"})
		return
	}
	nonce := make([]byte, gcm.NonceSize())
	if _, err := io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, nonce); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "crypto"})
		return
	}
	ciphertext := gcm.Seal(nil, nonce, plain, nil)
	ciphertext = append(nonce, ciphertext...)
	encoded := base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(ciphertext)
	row := map[string]interface{}{"username": c.PostForm("username"), "password": encoded}
	if err := db.Table("accounts").Create(row).Error; err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "save"})
		return
	}
	c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"status": "stored"})
}
